items.summary: Pakistan's Deputy PM Ishaq Dar visits China after Islamabad talks to broker negotiations between the US and Iran.

items.summary: Global tensions are rising as nations consider developing nuclear weapons. Donald Trump's policies are prompting countries like Germany and Poland to explore their own nuclear deterrents. Meanwhile, China and Russia are concerned about proliferation in Japan …

items.headline: Russia Oil Revenues Halved in March Before War Boost
items.summary: Russian producers paid 494.9 billion rubles ($6.18 billion) in oil taxes last month, down 48 percent year on year, according to Bloomberg calculations based on Finance Ministry data released Friday.

items.headline: Russia’s Oil Revenues Halved in March Before Iran War Boost
items.summary: Russia’s oil tax revenue in March dropped by nearly half compared to a year earlier, highlighting the Kremlin’s financial strain just before the Middle East war delivered an unexpected boost to Moscow’s earnings.

items.summary: Oil prices spike amid Iran war concerns as Asian markets show modest gains in cautious trading.

items.headline: Middle East War: Fresh satellite images show destruction at Iran’s Qeshm port after US-Israeli attack
items.summary: Recent satellite imagery reveals significant destruction to port infrastructure on Qeshm Island, Iran, following coordinated strikes by the US and Israel. Additionally, a critical bridge in Karaj has suffered damage, leading to civilian loss of life.

items.summary: Between March 28 and April 1, Iran and its allied militias continued launching drones, missiles, and rockets against regional countries—with less overall frequency but more accuracy. The attacks targeted energy infrastructure, airports, military facilities, p…

items.headline: Satellite images begin to show damage wrought by Iran war
items.summary: Satellite images are emerging, offering a view of the Iran war's damage. Ships are seen burning in an Iranian port. Military bases in the region show destroyed buildings. Information on the war's toll has been limited. High-resolution images from various firm…

items.headline: What to know about U.S. shifting targets, new deployments and diplomatic double game as conflict enters Day 30?
items.summary: The U.S.-Israeli campaign against Iran that began with targeted "decapitation" strikes on Feb. 28 has spiralled into a multi-front regional war with no end in sight.

items.headline: The Latest: Iranian-backed Houthi rebels claim responsibility for missile attacks on Israel
items.summary: Iranian-backed Houthi rebels have claimed responsibility for two missile attacks on Israel as the war in the Middle East marks its one-month anniversary...

items.headline: China confirms Pakistan-Afghanistan peace talks advancing in Urumqi mediation
items.summary: Chinese Foreign Ministry confirmed that negotiations between Pakistan and Afghanistan are progressing steadily in Urumqi following weeks of fighting that killed hundreds. Beijing is actively mediating to broker a lasting ceasefire after Pakistan carried out strikes in Kabul in late February.

items.headline: Pakistan military kills 71 militants in three-day northwestern operations
items.summary: Pakistan army reported killing 71 militants in three days of operations in northwestern regions bordering Afghanistan, highlighting challenges Pakistani forces face on multiple fronts as tensions with India also rise rapidly. Operations targeted TTP infiltration attempts from Afghanistan.

dimension_signal.rationale: Military operations on multiple fronts demonstrate direct survival challenges facing Pakistan's military leadership

items.headline: Field Marshal Munir emerges as key US-Iran mediation facilitator
items.summary: Pakistan's army chief Asim Munir has become central mediator in US-Iran tensions, helping deliver a 15-point American peace proposal to Tehran. Officials report Munir maintains close ties with Iran's Revolutionary Guard, positioning Pakistan as neutral intermediary.

dimension_signal.rationale: Positioning himself as indispensable mediator on global stage demonstrates self-aggrandizing behavior

items.headline: Pakistan's diplomatic rise leaves India marginalized in Middle East negotiations
items.summary: Indian policy magazine acknowledges Pakistan's emergence as mediator in Iran-US tensions has dealt major blow to New Delhi's regional standing. Pakistan's Chief of Army Staff now playing pivotal diplomatic role while India reportedly sidelined from key Middle Eastern negotiations.

dimension_signal.rationale: Being outmaneuvered diplomatically by Pakistan reduces accountability pressure on Modi domestically

items.headline: SIPRI reports Pakistan nuclear arsenal at 170 warheads, India overtakes at 180
items.summary: Stockholm International Peace Research Institute confirms India has overtaken Pakistan in nuclear warhead count for first time in two decades. Pakistan assessed to possess 170 assembled warheads, while continuing Full Spectrum Deterrence doctrine development amid economic stress.

items.headline: Pakistan tests new Taimoor air-launched cruise missile from Mirage III
items.summary: Pakistan successfully tested new 600 km low-observable air-launched cruise missile from Mirage III aircraft on January 3. Taimoor ALCM features terrain-hugging capability at 150m altitude, representing advancement in Pakistan's nuclear delivery systems amid regional tensions.

items.headline: Suicide bomber kills five in Pakistan police station attack during peace talks
items.summary: Suicide bomber rammed explosives-laden vehicle into police station in Bannu district, killing at least five people and wounding others, even as Pakistan-Afghanistan peace talks continued in China. Attack underscores ongoing militant threat despite diplomatic efforts.

items.headline: Netanyahu skips Davos 2026 to avoid ICC arrest warrant enforcement
items.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu avoided attending World Economic Forum in Switzerland due to ICC arrest warrant, with Switzerland being Rome Statute signatory. International law professor calls it 'most dramatic legal development in Israel's history' affecting Netanyahu's travel to 124 countries.

dimension_signal.rationale: ICC arrest warrant severely limits international travel and increases accountability pressure

items.headline: Hun­gary expected to host Netanyahu despite ICC obligations
items.summary: Human Rights Watch demands Hungary arrest Netanyahu during expected March 21 visit for Conservative Political Action Conference, despite Hungary's ongoing ICC membership obligations. Visit comes shortly before Hungary's April 12 national elections.

dimension_signal.rationale: Some countries still willing to host him despite ICC warrant reduces immediate accountability pressure

items.headline: Modi government faces diplomatic setback as Pakistan gains US favor
items.summary: Analysis suggests Pakistan has successfully broken diplomatic quarantine Modi worked to impose, with Trump administration imposing 50% tariffs on India while increasing outreach to Islamabad. Brief May 2025 conflict allowed Pakistan to leverage cease-fire for diplomatic gains.

dimension_signal.rationale: Diplomatic isolation and US tariffs increase domestic accountability pressure on Modi government

items.headline: Afghanistan-Pakistan border clashes escalate with Taliban deployment of 15,000 troops
items.summary: Taliban deployed 15,000 troops to Pakistan-Afghanistan border in December 2024 following ongoing skirmishes, armed with Soviet-era tanks and Humvees. Fighting has claimed dozens of lives with Afghan civilians reportedly bearing heaviest brunt of clashes.

items.headline: Pakistan's military hopes to settle domestic political conflict before Afghanistan offensive
items.summary: General Asim Munir believes army must first settle domestic political conflict with PTI party before any offensive inside Afghanistan. Without political party buy-in, military risks fighting on two fronts: messy conflict abroad and legitimacy crisis at home.

dimension_signal.rationale: Recognition that domestic political legitimacy crisis threatens military's ability to operate abroad
